ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Last day to register or update voter registration for the May 5, 2026 Ohio primary. Ohio requires registration 30 days before the election. Register online at MyVote Ohio or by mail/in-person at your county BOE.

Deadline to request an absentee (mail) ballot for the May 5, 2026 Ohio primary. Ohio mails unsolicited absentee ballot applications to all registered voters; returned applications are processed by county BOEs. Applications must be received by the county BOE by this date.

In-person early voting (absentee voting in-office) begins at all OH-09 county boards of elections approximately 28 days before Election Day. Voters may cast absentee ballots in person at the Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, and Williams county BOEs.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Ohio statewide primary election. Polls open 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM. OH-09 Republican primary results: Derek Merrin won the five-candidate race, certified as Republican nominee on June 7, 2026. Marcy Kaptur (D, incumbent) ran unopposed in the Democratic primary.

Under Senate Bill 293 (effective 2025), Ohio eliminated the four-day grace period. Absentee ballots must now be received by the county BOE no later than 7:30 PM on Election Day — postmarked-by-Election-Day is no longer sufficient. This change took effect in the May 2026 primary.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: County BOEs complete their official canvass of the May 5 primary within 30 days of election day. OH-09 county BOEs certify provisional and outstanding ballots, after which results are submitted to the Secretary of State.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.milestone: Secretary of State certifies primary — Derek Merrin certified GOP nominee
ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Ohio Secretary of State Frank LaRose certified the May 5, 2026 primary results on June 7, 2026, locking in Derek Merrin as the certified Republican nominee for OH-09 to face Rep. Marcy Kaptur in November.

Last day to register or update voter registration for the November 3, 2026 general election. Ohio requires registration 30 days before the election (October 4). Register online at MyVote Ohio (myaccount.ohiosos.gov), by mail, or in person at your county BOE.

ELECTION_EVENTS.milestone: Unsolicited absentee ballot applications mailed — general
ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Ohio's Controlling Board approved $2.5 million on June 9, 2026 for the Secretary of State to mail unsolicited absentee ballot applications (with pre-stamped return envelopes) to all ~8 million registered Ohio voters. Applications typically arrive in August. OH-09 voters in Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, and Williams counties will receive these packets.

Deadline to request an absentee (mail) ballot for the November 3 general election. Applications must be received by the county BOE (not postmarked) by this date — approximately 7 days before Election Day per Ohio law. Voters who received the SOS unsolicited application should return it promptly in early October to ensure ballot receipt in time.

In-person early voting (absentee voting in-office) begins at all OH-09 county BOEs approximately 28 days before Election Day. Voters may cast absentee ballots in person without needing a reason. Check each county BOE for specific hours. Under HB 458, in-person voters must present a qualifying photo ID.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: U.S. midterm general election. OH-09: Rep. Marcy Kaptur (D, incumbent) vs. Derek Merrin (R, Trump-endorsed) vs. Matthew Althaus (L). Polls open 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM. Voters must present qualifying photo ID (HB 458). Race rated Toss Up — rematch of the 2024 race Kaptur won by ~2,000 votes.

ELECTION_EVENTS.milestone: Absentee ballot receipt deadline — general (Election Day, 7:30 PM)
ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Under Ohio Senate Bill 293 (effective 2025), absentee ballots must be received by the county BOE by 7:30 PM on Election Day — the four-day grace period (formerly allowing ballots postmarked by Election Day to arrive up to 4 days later) has been eliminated. This strict deadline applies to the November 2026 general election, including the closely watched Kaptur–Merrin race.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Official county BOE drop boxes (one per county under HB 458) close at 7:30 PM on Election Day. Absentee ballots returned to drop boxes must be deposited by this time. Each of the seven OH-09 county BOEs maintains one official drop box at the BOE office.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: County BOEs must complete the official canvass within 30 days of election day (approximately December 3, 2026). During this period, provisional ballots, absentee ballots received before the deadline, and any challenged ballots are verified and counted. Results are then submitted to the SOS.
ELECTION_EVENTS.governingAuthority: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams County BOEs

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Ohio Secretary of State certifies official statewide results, including the OH-09 general election outcome, approximately 40 days after election day. This is the final official result that determines the winner of the Kaptur–Merrin contest.

RULES.topic: Photo voter ID required at polls (HB 458)
RULES.currentRule: Ohio requires a qualifying photo ID to vote in person. Acceptable IDs: Ohio driver license or state ID, U.S. passport, U.S. military ID, Ohio National Guard ID, or U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs ID. The ID must show the voter's name and photo; address is not required. Voters without ID may cast a provisional ballot and present qualifying ID by the 7th day after the election.
RULES.whyItMatters: All OH-09 in-person voters (at polls and early voting/in-office absentee at county BOEs) must present qualifying photo ID. Ohio no longer accepts utility bills or other non-photo documents for in-person voting. Voters without qualifying ID may use a provisional ballot.
RULES.detail: Ohio HB 458 (enacted 2023). Ohio Revised Code § 3505.18. A free Ohio ID is available for eligible voters from the Ohio BMV.

RULES.currentRule: Any qualified Ohio voter may vote by absentee ballot, either by mail or in person, without stating a reason. To vote by mail, a voter must submit an absentee ballot application to their county board of elections; applications may be downloaded, requested from the board of elections, or obtained from other authorized sources. Once the board of elections receives and processes a valid application, it mails an absentee ballot to the voter. The Ohio Secretary of State does not state on its elections website that it mails unsolicited absentee ballot applications to all registered voters before each major election or that applications for the November 2026 election will be mailed in August 2026.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 voters — including seniors and rural residents in Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, and Williams counties — can vote by mail without a reason. The SOS program sends pre-addressed, pre-stamped applications directly to voters.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code § 3509.01 et seq. The SOS unsolicited application program was re-authorized for November 2026 via Ohio Controlling Board approval of $2.5M on June 9, 2026.

RULES.topic: Voter registration deadline — 30 days before election
RULES.currentRule: Ohio requires voter registration to be received (not postmarked) by the county BOE 30 days before the election. For the November 3, 2026 general election, the deadline is October 4, 2026. Registration is available online via MyVote Ohio, by mail, or in person at the county BOE or participating agencies.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 voters who are not yet registered or who have moved within Ohio must complete their registration by October 4, 2026. There is no same-day registration in Ohio.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code § 3503.19. Online registration via myaccount.ohiosos.gov. Ohio does not offer same-day voter registration; no conditional registration option exists.

RULES.topic: Ballot drop boxes — one per county (HB 458 restriction)
RULES.currentRule: Under Ohio HB 458 (enacted 2023), each county is limited to one official ballot drop box, located at the county Board of Elections office. Drop boxes are open during BOE office hours and on Election Day through 7:30 PM. Third parties may not operate drop boxes; only the county BOE may administer them.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 spans seven counties, each with exactly one drop box. In Lucas County (Toledo), this single drop box serves hundreds of thousands of voters. The restriction significantly limits access compared to pre-2023 drop box policy, particularly in densely populated Toledo.
RULES.detail: Ohio HB 458 (2023). Drop box location at each county BOE office. Specific hours vary by county — check the relevant county BOE for holiday hours and extended Election Day hours.

RULES.topic: Absentee ballot receipt deadline — no grace period (SB 293)
RULES.currentRule: Ohio Senate Bill 293 (enacted 2023, effective for 2025 elections) eliminated the four-day grace period for mail absentee ballots. Absentee ballots must now be received by the county BOE no later than 7:30 PM on Election Day. A ballot that arrives even one minute after polls close on Election Day — regardless of postmark — will not be counted.
RULES.whyItMatters: This is a major rule change directly affecting OH-09's high absentee voting population, particularly seniors and disabled voters in Toledo-area Lucas County. In the May 2026 primary, 1,564 ballots were rejected statewide under this rule. Voters are advised to mail ballots at least 7–10 days early or use the drop box at their county BOE.
RULES.detail: Ohio SB 293 (2023). Ohio Revised Code § 3509.05. ACLU of Ohio has urged voters to treat Election Day as the hard deadline for ballot return. The rule was first applied in the May 2026 primary, resulting in over 1,500 rejected ballots statewide.

RULES.currentRule: Ohio county BOEs are required to compare the signature on the absentee ballot return envelope against the voter's signature on file. If a signature is missing or does not match, the county must notify the voter. Under Ohio law, voters have an opportunity to cure a defective signature (absentee ballot affirmation) within a specified period after notification.
RULES.whyItMatters: Signature issues are a leading cause of absentee ballot rejection in OH-09. Voters should sign the ballot return envelope exactly as their registered signature. Voters who receive a cure notice must respond promptly.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code § 3509.07 (absentee ballot envelope), § 3509.08. Voters are notified by the county BOE; cure procedures and timelines vary by county. Confirm specific cure window with Lucas, Ottawa, or Erie County BOE.

RULES.currentRule: Ohio voters who cannot have their eligibility confirmed at the polls — e.g., those who did not present a qualifying photo ID, voters at the wrong precinct, voters who requested an absentee ballot but appear at the polls in person — must be offered a provisional ballot. Provisional ballots are verified by county BOEs during the canvass period and counted if the voter's eligibility is confirmed.
RULES.whyItMatters: In the close Kaptur–Merrin race, provisional ballot counts from OH-09's seven counties could be determinative. Voters who lack qualifying ID on Election Day should use a provisional ballot and have until 7 days after the election to present the required ID to their county BOE.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code § 3505.181. A voter using a provisional ballot due to ID issues must provide qualifying ID to the county BOE by the 7th day after Election Day. Check provisional ballot status at MyVote Ohio.

RULES.topic: Ban on third-party absentee ballot return
RULES.currentRule: Ohio law prohibits third parties from collecting and returning voters' completed absentee ballots. Voters must return their own ballot by mail, in person to the county BOE, or via the official county BOE drop box. Campaign workers, political organizations, and other third parties may not possess or submit another voter's completed ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 voters and campaign volunteers should not rely on third-party ballot collection drives. Any completed absentee ballot must be returned only by the voter themselves, or mailed directly.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code § 3599.20. Prohibited conduct; applicable statewide. Violators may be subject to criminal penalties.

RULES.topic: Voter ID constitutional amendment on November ballot (Issue 3 / SJR 10)
RULES.currentRule: The Ohio General Assembly passed Senate Joint Resolution 10 in June 2026 — the Senate adopted it June 3, 2026 (22–9); the House adopted it June 10, 2026 (61–27), both clearing the required three-fifths supermajority. The measure will appear on the November 3, 2026 ballot as a statewide constitutional amendment to enshrine Ohio's existing HB 458 in-person photo ID requirement in the state Constitution. The amendment does not change how ballots are cast in November 2026 — current photo ID law (HB 458) remains in effect regardless of the outcome. If passed by voters, it would prevent future legislative or judicial repeal without another statewide vote. The amendment covers in-person photo ID only; a separate bill, HB 472, would require a photocopy of ID for mail ballots starting with the November 2027 elections. Governor's signature not required for joint resolutions.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 voters will encounter this constitutional amendment on the same ballot as the congressional race. The measure could affect overall turnout and enthusiasm, and its passage would lock in the photo ID requirement at the constitutional level. Monitor for finalized ballot language and official number assignment.
RULES.detail: Ohio SJR 10, passed June 10, 2026. Ballot measure designation pending finalization by Ohio SOS. Bipartisan election officials including SOS Frank LaRose warned the companion HB 472 (mail ID photo requirement) was rushed without implementation planning.

RULES.topic: New photo ID requirement for mail ballots — takes effect 2027 (not 2026)
RULES.currentRule: Ohio HB 472 (passed June 10–11, 2026) requires voters to enclose a photocopy of a state-issued photo ID when returning a mail absentee ballot. This requirement does NOT apply to the November 2026 general election — it takes effect ahead of the November 2027 election. The November 3, 2026 election (including OH-09) operates under current mail voting rules.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 voters do NOT need to include a photo ID copy with their absentee ballot in November 2026. However, county BOEs (especially Lucas County) must begin planning for significant administrative and outreach changes for 2027. Senior and disabled voters in northwest Ohio — who vote absentee at high rates — will be most affected when this rule takes effect.
RULES.detail: Ohio HB 472 (2026). Effective for elections after November 2026. Bipartisan election officials and SOS LaRose warned the 48-hour legislative timeline was dangerously rushed. Implementation guidance from SOS pending.

RULES.topic: Polling place hours — 6:30 AM to 7:30 PM
RULES.currentRule: Ohio polls are open from 6:30 AM to 7:30 PM on Election Day. Any voter in line at 7:30 PM is entitled to vote. Polling locations are assigned by precinct; voters must vote at their assigned polling place or use a provisional ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: OH-09 voters in Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, and Williams counties should verify their assigned polling location before November 3, 2026 at MyVote Ohio (myaccount.ohiosos.gov). Voting at the wrong polling place will result in a provisional ballot that may not be counted.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code § 3501.32. Polling hours are statewide standard. Verify your precinct polling location using MyVote Ohio before Election Day.

RULES.currentRule: Ohio county BOEs must complete the official canvass within 21–30 days of Election Day. The Secretary of State certifies results after county certification. For the November 3 general election, county canvass completion is expected by approximately December 3, 2026, with SOS certification approximately December 14, 2026.
RULES.whyItMatters: In a close race like Kaptur vs. Merrin — which was decided by ~2,000 votes in 2024 — provisional ballots and late absentee ballots resolved during the canvass period may shift the final result. Official results are not final until after the canvass.
RULES.detail: Ohio Revised Code §§ 3505.32, 3505.33. County canvasses must be completed before state certification. Each of the seven OH-09 county BOEs has its own canvass schedule; confirm specific dates with each county.

RESOURCES.label: Ohio Secretary of State — Elections portal
RESOURCES.description: Official Ohio elections hub: voter registration, election calendar, county BOE directory, absentee voting, and statewide voting rules. Authoritative source for OH-09 election deadlines and procedures.

RESOURCES.label: MyVote Ohio — voter registration portal
RESOURCES.description: Check your voter registration status, update your address, request an absentee ballot, find your polling location, and track your absentee ballot. Registration deadline for November 2026 general: October 4, 2026.

RESOURCES.label: Ohio Absentee Ballot Tracker (MyVote Ohio)
RESOURCES.description: Track the status of your absentee ballot request and returned ballot via MyVote Ohio. Given the elimination of the grace period under SB 293, voters should confirm receipt of their ballot well before 7:30 PM on Election Day.

RESOURCES.label: Lucas County Board of Elections
RESOURCES.description: Official elections page for Lucas County voters (Toledo, Oregon, Sylvania, Maumee area) — the most populous county in OH-09. Absentee ballot requests, drop box location, polling places, and canvass updates. Phone: (419) 213-4001.

RESOURCES.label: Ottawa County Board of Elections
RESOURCES.description: Official elections page for Ottawa County voters (Port Clinton, Marblehead, Put-in-Bay area) along the Lake Erie shore in OH-09. Absentee ballot requests and polling place information.

RESOURCES.label: Erie County Board of Elections
RESOURCES.description: Official elections page for Erie County voters (Sandusky, Huron area) in OH-09. Absentee ballot requests, polling locations, and election results.

RESOURCES.label: Ballotpedia — OH-09 2026 election
RESOURCES.description: Nonpartisan reference page for OH-09's 2026 election: Kaptur vs. Merrin candidate profiles, primary results, race ratings (Toss Up), and campaign finance summary.

RESOURCES.label: Ohio SOS — County Boards of Elections directory
RESOURCES.description: Complete directory of all 88 Ohio county BOEs with contact information, including all seven OH-09 counties: Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, and Williams.

RESOURCES.label: Ohio BMV — Free Voter ID
RESOURCES.description: Eligible Ohio voters who lack a qualifying photo ID for voting can obtain a free Ohio non-driver ID from the Ohio Bureau of Motor Vehicles. Required for in-person voting under HB 458. Available at BMV locations throughout OH-09 counties.

UPDATES.title: OH-09 dashboard initialized — 14 sources, 16 events, 12 rules, 6 news items wired
UPDATES.newValue: Initial build June 11, 2026. Sources: Ohio SOS, ORC Title 35, Lucas/Ottawa/Erie/Fulton/Defiance county BOEs, Ballotpedia OH-09, Toledo Blade, Signal Ohio, 10TV, Ohio Star, WYSO Public Radio, plus MyVote voter portal. All election events and voting rules researched from Ohio SOS, ORC Title 35, and news sources, but all marked needsReview/unconfirmed pending authoritative confirmation from each county BOE. Key Ohio-specific rules documented: HB 458 photo ID, SB 293 absentee receipt deadline (no grace period), drop box restriction (1 per county per HB 458), HB 472 future mail ID requirement (2027+), and voter ID constitutional amendment on November ballot.

UPDATES.title: OH-09 primary certified — Marcy Kaptur (D) vs. Derek Merrin (R) set for November general
UPDATES.newValue: Rep. Marcy Kaptur (D, 41-year incumbent) vs. Derek Merrin (R, former state rep., Trump-endorsed May 21) vs. Matthew Althaus (L). Ohio SOS certified primary on June 7, 2026. Merrin won a five-candidate Republican primary on May 5 and was locked in as GOP nominee. Race rated Toss Up by major forecasters; rematch of 2024 race Kaptur won by ~2,000 votes. Libertarian Matthew Althaus also on general election ballot.

UPDATES.title: All rules and event dates flagged for county-level verification — QA pending
UPDATES.newValue: 12 voting rules and 16 election events all marked needsReview: true, confidence: unconfirmed (lastVerified 2026-06-11). Priority review items: (1) Confirm specific absentee application deadlines with Lucas, Ottawa, Erie county BOEs; (2) Verify drop box hours and locations for all 7 counties; (3) Confirm canvass schedules per county; (4) Verify photo ID constitutional amendment ballot designation and language; (5) Confirm SB 293 no-grace-period rule is correctly stated for county BOE communications.

CONFLICTS.factInQuestion: Drop box access hours and availability for OH-09 county BOEs under HB 458 one-drop-box-per-county rule
CONFLICTS.sourceAClaim: Ohio HB 458 (ORC § 3509.05) limits each county to one official drop box located at the county BOE. The statute specifies the drop box must be accessible during BOE office hours, but does not mandate 24/7 access or uniform extended hours across all counties.
CONFLICTS.sourceBClaim: Lucas County BOE (serving Toledo's population of ~400,000+ registered voters) may publish its own drop box hours and procedures that differ from smaller OH-09 counties like Williams or Henry. Individual county BOE websites are the only authoritative source for specific drop box hours, and county-level guidance may contradict or expand upon the sparse statutory framework.
CONFLICTS.whyItMatters: OH-09 spans seven counties with widely varying populations. Lucas County's single drop box serves far more voters than, say, Williams County's. If voters rely on county BOE websites for drop box hours and those hours differ from statewide SOS guidance — or if a county BOE page is outdated — voters may miss the 7:30 PM Election Day deadline. This ambiguity was flagged during the SB 293 grace-period discussion as a potential disenfranchisement risk.
CONFLICTS.suggestedReviewAction: Confirm drop box hours for all seven OH-09 county BOEs (Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, Williams) directly from each county BOE's 2026 general election page. Note any counties that do not post extended Election Day hours or clear signage on the one-drop-box location. Update the drop box rule and event entries with county-specific verified information before publish.

NEWS.headline: Ohio Adds New Photo ID Requirement for Mail Voting Starting in 2027
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Ohio's Republican-majority legislature fast-tracked House Bill 472 on June 10–11, 2026, requiring voters to enclose a photocopy of their state-issued photo ID when returning a mail ballot — a major change for northwest Ohio seniors and disabled voters who have historically used absentee voting at high rates. The law takes effect ahead of the November 2027 election, leaving the November 2026 contest (including the OH-09 Kaptur–Merrin rematch) under current rules. Lucas County and other northwest Ohio boards of elections will need to prepare significant administrative and outreach changes. Bipartisan election officials statewide, including Ohio SOS Frank LaRose, warned the 48-hour timeline was dangerously rushed.

NEWS.headline: Ohio Secretary of State Certifies May 5 Primary; Launches Election Integrity Transparency Portal
NEWS.sourceName: Ohio Secretary of State Frank LaRose (official Week in Review, ohiosos.gov)
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Secretary of State Frank LaRose officially certified the May 5, 2026 Ohio Primary Election results on June 7 following completion of the statewide canvass — the administrative step that locks in Derek Merrin as the certified Republican nominee to face Rep. Marcy Kaptur in OH-09 this November. LaRose also announced a new public-access online resource for Election Integrity Unit cases and Ohio Election Integrity Commission decisions, and deployed a new cybersecurity initiative. These updates directly affect Lucas County's Board of Elections, which administers elections for the largest county in OH-09.

NEWS.headline: 1,564 Mail-In Ballots Tossed Statewide Under Ohio's New No-Grace-Period Rule in May Primary
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Ohio's first election under Senate Bill 293 — which eliminated the four-day grace period for late mail-in ballots — resulted in 1,564 ballots being invalidated statewide in the May 5 primary. This rule directly affects OH-09 voters in Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, and surrounding counties who rely on absentee voting, particularly seniors and military/overseas voters. ACLU of Ohio urged voters to either mail ballots early or drop them off in person at their local board of elections before 7:30 p.m. on Election Day. The new strict deadline will apply to the November 2026 general election, including the closely watched Kaptur–Merrin contest.

NEWS.headline: Ohio Will Again Mail Absentee Ballot Applications to All 8 Million Registered Voters Ahead of November 2026 Election
NEWS.relevanceSummary: The Ohio Controlling Board approved $2.5 million on June 9, 2026 for the Secretary of State to mail unsolicited absentee ballot applications — including pre-stamped return envelopes — to all roughly 8 million registered Ohio voters ahead of the November election. Registered voters in Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, Fulton, Defiance, Henry, and Williams counties (all in OH-09) will receive these packets, typically in August; returned applications will result in blank ballots being mailed in early October. Mail voting has declined sharply since 2020, which may shape turnout dynamics in the razor-thin Kaptur–Merrin general election.

NEWS.headline: Ohio Republicans Put Voter ID Constitutional Amendment on November Ballot
NEWS.relevanceSummary: In a rapid pre-recess push backed by President Trump, Ohio lawmakers passed Senate Joint Resolution 10 on June 10, 2026, placing a constitutional amendment enshrining existing in-person voter photo ID requirements on the November 3 ballot — the same day OH-09 voters will decide the Kaptur–Merrin race. Voters in Lucas, Ottawa, Erie, and surrounding counties will simultaneously vote on Issue 3 (expected ballot name), and the contested measure could affect overall turnout and enthusiasm in the competitive district. Secretary of State Frank LaRose and bipartisan election officials warned the companion mail ID bill (HB 472) was rushed without adequate implementation planning.

NEWS.headline: Derek Merrin Wins Republican Nomination for OH-09 Congressional Seat, Setting Up Kaptur Rematch
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Former Ohio state Rep. Derek Merrin won a competitive five-candidate Republican primary on May 5, 2026 and was certified as the GOP nominee for OH-09 following Ohio's official canvass completed June 7. The win sets up a direct rematch against 41-year incumbent Rep. Marcy Kaptur (D-Toledo), who narrowly defeated Merrin in 2024 by roughly 2,000 votes. Merrin received a Trump endorsement on May 21 and leads Kaptur 47%–43% in a Republican internal poll, with the race rated Toss Up by major forecasters. An estimated $39 million in total spending has flowed through the district over the two-year cycle, making it one of Ohio's most watched races in 2026.

LAST_REFRESH.summary: Automated refresh completed 2026-07-01 for OH-09. Sources checked: 23. LLM verifications: 15. 1 rule/source content change + 3 lastVerified bumps on unchanged sources. 1 conflict flagged for human review.

WEEKLY_REPORT.summary: Initial build week of June 8–14, 2026. OH-09 dashboard created post-primary. Kaptur (D) vs. Merrin (R) vs. Althaus (L) set for November 3. Ohio SOS certified primary June 7. General election timeline covers registration (Oct 4), unsolicited absentee apps mailed (~Aug), absentee app deadline (~Oct 27), early voting (Oct 6), Election Day (Nov 3), absentee receipt deadline (Nov 3, 7:30 PM — no grace period per SB 293), canvass (~Dec 3), SOS certification (~Dec 14). 1 conflict flagged: drop box hours/access ambiguity across 7 counties. All sources verified. All events and rules flagged needsReview pending county-specific 2026 confirmation.
